events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll create the required JSON file directly. The scenario gives enough information to classify missing jack studs at inspection as a structural framing defect against IRC wall/header support requirements.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && cat > /app/answer.json <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"tested/inspected\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 95,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.92,
  \"event_time\": 0,
  \"rate\": 0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Missing jack studs at framed opening; header/beam bearing support is incomplete.\",
    \"Opening framing does not match prescriptive IRC wall framing requirements or the available drawing/checklist.\",
    \"Load path is interrupted and may overstress adjacent king studs, fasteners, header, or finishes.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Do not approve or cover the framing until corrected and reinspected.\",
    \"Install required jack/trimmer studs or engineered alternate support per approved plans and IRC requirements.\",
    \"Verify header size, bearing length, fastening, and load path after repair.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"Missing jack studs are a structural framing defect because headers over openings require proper bearing support through jack/trimmer studs or an approved engineered equivalent. At the tested/inspected stage this is not acceptable for acceptance or concealment.\",
  \"references\": [\"IRC R502\", \"IRC R602\"]
}
EOF
python3 -m json.tool /app/answer.json >/dev/null && ls -l /app/answer.json"
→ /app
total 8
drwxrwxrwx 1 root root 4096 Jul  5 06:10 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 Jul  6 22:44 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 agent agent 1239 Jul  6 22:44 /app/answer.json
